svg.title: YouTube HLD with Fallback Mechanisms
svg.desc: Complete YouTube high-level design with fallback and crash recovery for upload pipeline, transcoding, CDN, streaming, Redis, PostgreSQL, Kafka, and analytics.

g.text: Auth, rate limit per channel, route
Chunked upload: reassemble in object storage (S3)
Generate videoId, write metadata to PostgreSQL
Step 1: Validate format + size + content policy
Step 5: Content moderation (AI — hate / violence / copyright)
Step 6: Index in Elasticsearch + mark PUBLISHED
All quality variants distributed to edge nodes globally
Viewer in India → India edge, US → US edge
Push + email notification to subscribed users

svg.text: — fallback zone 1: upload service / processing pipeline crash —

g.text: Crash scenario A — upload service or transcoding fails
Fetch segments from CDN edge node (~5ms latency)

svg.text: — fallback zone 2: streaming / CDN edge failure —

g.text: Crash scenario B — CDN edge node or ABR stream failure

svg.text: — fallback zone 3: Redis / metadata service crash —

g.text: Crash scenario C — Redis down or metadata service fails
Two parallel requests on page load

svg.text: — fallback zone 4: home feed / search / recommendation crash —

g.text: Crash scenario D — feed, search or ML recommendation fails
1,000+ edge nodes globally — video from nearest node
Each edge caches hot videos (top 20% = 80% of traffic)
Cache miss → regional cache → origin S3
DASH / HLS: 2-second segments allow mid-stream quality switch
Segments pre-cached based on trending video prediction

svg.text: — fallback zone 5: CDN origin / S3 failure —

g.text: Crash scenario E — S3 region or CDN origin outage
Kafka stream: views, watch-time, CTR, likes, comments
Cassandra: time-series metrics per video / channel / day
Ad revenue calculated on CPM / CPC per 1000 views

svg.text: — fallback zone 6: Kafka / Flink / Cassandra / analytics failure —

g.text: Crash scenario F — analytics pipeline or data stores down

svg.text: — fallback zone 7: PostgreSQL primary DB crash —

g.text: Crash scenario G — primary PostgreSQL down
YouTube vs Netflix: different scale challenges
YouTube: UGC — scale challenge is upload + processing pipeline (500hr/min)
Netflix: licensed content — scale challenge is global CDN + personalised recommendation
YouTube transcoding requires distributed GPU clusters; Netflix pre-encodes finite catalogue
